description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a flat, rectangular base with several dark, glassy solar panels mounted on top. The panels are arranged in a single row and are slightly elevated above the base, supported by a metallic frame. The base appears to house additional components, possibly including electrical equipment or batteries, and is finished in a combination of muted gray and pinkish tones. Small protrusions and details on the base suggest the presence of connection points or minor control elements. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on maximizing solar exposure while maintaining a low profile. The structure is likely intended for ground installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas.
description: A compact, rectangular solar energy unit with a row of dark solar panels on a muted gray and pink base, featuring metallic supports and minor control elements.
description_view_0: From this top-down view, the rectangular arrangement of the solar panels and the muted color base are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elevation of the solar panels above the base and shows the metallic support structure.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back edge of the panels and additional details on the base, including possible connection points.
description_view_3: This opposite side view shows the panel tilt and the structure of the base, with visible protrusions and minor components.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are flat or nearly flat, with a slight tilt of approximately 10 degrees, orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic (likely aluminum) frame supporting the panels above a rectangular base.
attribute_array_configuration: Single linear row of 3 panels, closely spaced on a rectangular base.
